name: finresearch-cli
description: Machine-readable Finance CLI command metadata for LLM agents, MCP adapters, and plugin wrappers.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

data.description: Command-specific payload. Null when ok is false.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

side_effect_levels.pure_calculation: No network, no filesystem read, no mutation.
side_effect_levels.local_file_read: Reads local files supplied by the caller. Does not mutate files.
side_effect_levels.network_read_only: Reads remote public/provider data. Does not mutate external state.
side_effect_levels.local_or_network_read: Reads a local path or remote URL supplied by the caller. Does not mutate files or external state.

trust_policy.market_data: Never claim live market data without provider/source and timestamp/date fields when available.
trust_policy.source_truth: Treat Yahoo, FMP, SEC, GDELT, transcripts, and company IR as source-specific records, not ground truth.
trust_policy.credentials: API keys are read from environment variables at runtime and are not written by the CLI.

playbooks.when: The user asks for a metric or table value from a company's latest 10-K.
failure_modes: If no XBRL row matches, fall back to filings.reports then document.scan.
If document.scan finds multiple matches, use document.window around each candidate and cite offsets.

playbooks.when: The user asks what happened around a dated price move.
failure_modes: If no dated evidence appears, say the CLI found no direct evidence rather than inferring causality.
Always cite provider, date, and URL/accession when available.

playbooks.when: The user asks for market ideas from predefined Yahoo screens.
failure_modes: If a screen returns fewer rows than requested, keep the returned count and source.
Do not describe screen membership as a recommendation.

playbooks.when: The user asks to test a strategy over explicit symbols and dates.
failure_modes: If provider data is unavailable, fail clearly with the provider error.
Preserve strategy name, symbols, dates, params, and JSON output.

commands.name: backtest.describe
commands.description: Describe a backtest strategy and its parameters

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Backtest metrics, trades, plots, or payload metadata.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads remote public/provider data. Does not mutate external state.
commands.rate_limit_notes: Provider data downloads may be rate limited.

agent.use_when: Use for repeatable strategy checks, payload construction, and VectorBT result summaries.
agent.avoid_when: Do not present backtests as live trading recommendations.

commands.name: backtest.run
commands.description: Run a VectorBT strategy backtest
commands.usage: finance backtest.run STRATEGY SYMBOLS START_DATE END_DATE [params='{}' strategy_file=./rule.py]
examples: finance backtest.run sma_cross AAPL 2020-01-01 2024-12-31 fast=20 slow=100
finance backtest.run custom AAPL 2020-01-01 2024-12-31 strategy_file=./rule.py params='{}'

commands.name: document.ocr
commands.description: Run PaddleOCR/PP-StructureV3 OCR fallback on a local or remote document
notes: Prefer document.read or document.scan for text-based PDFs.
Uses the default Finance CLI OCR stack.
Use as fallback for scanned or image-heavy documents.

source.description: Source key, path, or URL depending on command.
Source key, path, or URL depending on command.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Document text, blocks, matches, tables, OCR, or window metadata.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads a local path or remote URL supplied by the caller. Does not mutate files or external state.

agent.use_when: Use as fallback for scanned or image-heavy PDFs after document.read/scan cannot extract text.

commands.name: document.read
commands.description: Extract native PDF or HTML text and layout/search blocks
notes: Lightweight first-pass parser for text-based PDFs and HTML filings; does not run OCR.
Returns page text plus positioned or offset-bearing blocks for downstream matching and analysis.

source.description: Source key, path, or URL depending on command.
Source key, path, or URL depending on command.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Document text, blocks, matches, tables, OCR, or window metadata.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads a local path or remote URL supplied by the caller. Does not mutate files or external state.

agent.use_when: Use for first-pass text extraction from a known PDF or SEC HTML URL.

commands.name: document.scan
commands.description: Scan document text/layout for configured topics or literal queries with RapidFuzz
commands.usage: finance document.scan SOURCE|source=PATH_OR_URL [query=... topics=risk,disclosure format=pdf|html match=fuzzy|all_terms threshold=80 max_chars=12000 max_pages=5 limit=50 window=0 start_char=0 end_char=0]
examples: finance document.scan url=https://www.sec.gov/.../filing.htm format=html query='Operating lease costs' max_chars=0 window=1200
finance document.scan url=https://www.sec.gov/.../filing.htm format=html match=all_terms threshold=100 query='Receivables net Total current assets' max_chars=0
finance document.scan url=https://www.sec.gov/.../filing.htm format=html start_char=122000 query='Accounts payable'
notes: Uses PyMuPDF for native PDF layout, BeautifulSoup for HTML text, and RapidFuzz for deterministic fuzzy matching.
Known topics include disclosure, risk, financial_reporting, portfolio, and guidance.
Unknown topics are treated as literal fuzzy queries.
Use match=all_terms threshold=100 for table-style queries where every meaningful query word should appear.
Use start_char/end_char to restrict a follow-up scan to a known section or window.
HTML matches include char offsets and match IDs that can be passed to document.window.

query.description: Literal phrase or table text to find.

topics.description: Known topic names or literal queries.

max_chars.description: Maximum text chars to read; 0 means unbounded in current readers.

max_pages.description: Maximum pages for PDFs; 0 means all pages.

start_char.description: Start offset for a bounded scan.

end_char.description: End offset for a bounded scan.

query.description: Literal phrase or table text to find.

start_char.description: Start offset for a bounded scan.

end_char.description: End offset for a bounded scan.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads a local path or remote URL supplied by the caller. Does not mutate files or external state.

agent.use_when: Use when the user needs phrase, table, or topic discovery inside a filing or document.

commands.name: document.tables
commands.description: Extract compact table previews from text-based PDFs with Camelot
commands.usage: finance document.tables SOURCE|source=PATH_OR_URL [pages=1-end flavor=stream|lattice max_tables=20 max_rows=25]
notes: Uses the default Finance CLI table extraction stack.
Use flavor=stream for whitespace-separated tables and flavor=lattice for ruled-line tables.
Returns compact row previews with page, shape, accuracy, and truncation metadata.

source.description: Source key, path, or URL depending on command.
Source key, path, or URL depending on command.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Document text, blocks, matches, tables, OCR, or window metadata.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads a local path or remote URL supplied by the caller. Does not mutate files or external state.

agent.use_when: Use for table previews in text-based PDFs when text windows are not enough.

commands.name: document.window
commands.description: Read a bounded text window around a document offset or scan match ID
commands.usage: finance document.window SOURCE|source=PATH_OR_URL [format=pdf|html start_char=0|match_id=char_START_END chars=4000 direction=around|next|previous]
examples: finance document.window url=https://www.sec.gov/.../filing.htm format=html start_char=52000 chars=4000
finance document.window url=https://www.sec.gov/.../filing.htm format=html match_id=char_52000_52200 direction=next chars=4000
notes: Designed for follow-up reading after document.scan.
Use direction=next or direction=previous to move through a table or section without re-scanning.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Document text, blocks, matches, tables, OCR, or window metadata.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads a local path or remote URL supplied by the caller. Does not mutate files or external state.

agent.use_when: Use after document.scan to read around a stable char offset or match_id without re-scanning the full document.

commands.name: estimates.compare
commands.description: Compare user assumptions against explicit consensus inputs
commands.usage: finance estimates.compare [SYMBOL] revenue=2.2B consensus_revenue=2.0B eps=0.50 consensus_eps=0.45 fiscal_year=2027
examples: finance estimates.compare IOT revenue=2.2B consensus_revenue=2.0B eps=0.50 consensus_eps=0.45 fiscal_year=2027
notes: No network calls. Compares only values explicitly supplied by the caller.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Consensus estimate or comparison result.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: No network, no filesystem read, no mutation.

agent.use_when: Use for explicit estimate comparison math and structured consensus result shaping.

commands.name: estimates.consensus
commands.description: Fetch FMP analyst consensus estimates when configured
notes: Requires FMP_API_KEY. Makes one short FMP request and returns a structured configuration error when unconfigured.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Consensus estimate or comparison result.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: No network, no filesystem read, no mutation.

agent.use_when: Use for explicit estimate comparison math and structured consensus result shaping.

commands.name: ir.presentations
commands.description: Discover IR and investor day presentations from SEC 8-K Exhibit 99 filings
notes: Scans recent 8-K filings for Exhibit 99 files with presentation or slides keywords.
source=auto uses SEC first, then company IR fallback when SEC finds no candidates.
source=all combines SEC and company IR candidates.
Press releases and earnings releases are filtered unless a distinct deck/slides signal is present.
confidence: high = strong presentation signal, medium = weaker or conflicting signal.
kind: investor_day | earnings_presentation | ir_presentation | exhibit_99
Use ir.read url=URL to extract text from a candidate.

source.description: Source key, path, or URL depending on command.
Source key, path, or URL depending on command.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Investor-presentation discovery or text extraction result.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads remote public/provider data. Does not mutate external state.

agent.use_when: Use to discover or read investor presentations from SEC exhibits and company IR pages.

commands.name: ir.read
commands.description: Extract text from an IR presentation exhibit URL
notes: HTML exhibits/pages are fetched and parsed to plain text.
PDF extraction uses pypdf and returns page-level text when possible.
ocr=auto or ocr=force uses the default PaddleOCR/PP-StructureV3 stack.
Pass the url from ir.presentations output.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Investor-presentation discovery or text extraction result.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads remote public/provider data. Does not mutate external state.

agent.use_when: Use to discover or read investor presentations from SEC exhibits and company IR pages.

commands.name: news.analyze
commands.description: Analyze news volume, tone, context, or geography
commands.usage: finance news.analyze analysis=timeline|tone|context|geo|doc [query=TEXT | symbol=SYMBOL | sector=SECTOR] [mode=MODE max_records=50 timespan=30D|1W|1M|24H date=YYYY-MM-DD start_date=YYYY-MM-DD end_date=YYYY-MM-DD start_datetime=YYYYMMDDHHMMSS end_datetime=YYYYMMDDHHMMSS]
examples: finance news.analyze query='NVIDIA export controls' analysis=context max_records=5 timespan=24h
finance news.analyze symbol=IOT analysis=timeline start_date=2026-03-03 end_date=2026-03-09
finance news.analyze query=FOOD_SECURITY analysis=geo max_records=3 timespan=1h
notes: Use this only when you need trend, tone, context, geo, or raw DOC analysis.
Use timespan for relative lookback from now, such as 30D, 1W, 1M, 24H, or 90min.
date/start_date/end_date are preferred for routine use; datetime inputs provide precision controls.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: News records or analysis result.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads remote public/provider data. Does not mutate external state.
commands.rate_limit_notes: News providers can rate limit and can return sparse windows.

agent.use_when: Use for source-attributed news search and GDELT analysis windows.

commands.name: news.search
commands.description: Search finance news through GDELT
commands.usage: finance news.search [query=TEXT | symbol=SYMBOL | sector=SECTOR] [max_records=50 timespan=30D|1W|1M|24H date=YYYY-MM-DD start_date=YYYY-MM-DD end_date=YYYY-MM-DD start_datetime=YYYYMMDDHHMMSS end_datetime=YYYYMMDDHHMMSS]
examples: finance news.search query='NVIDIA export controls' timespan=24h
finance news.search symbol=IOT start_date=2026-03-03 end_date=2026-03-09 max_records=5
notes: Use date for one full day, or start_date/end_date for a full-day range.
Use timespan for relative lookback from now, such as 30D, 1W, 1M, 24H, or 90min.
Use start_datetime/end_datetime only when you need second-level precision.
Use either timespan or fixed date/date-time inputs, not both.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: News records or analysis result.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads remote public/provider data. Does not mutate external state.
commands.rate_limit_notes: News providers can rate limit and can return sparse windows.

agent.use_when: Use for source-attributed news search and GDELT analysis windows.

commands.name: price.context
commands.description: Return a source-linked evidence timeline around a date
commands.usage: finance price.context SYMBOL date=YYYY-MM-DD [lookback=3D news_limit=5 filing_limit=80 transcript_limit=12]
examples: finance price.context NVDA date=2025-01-27 lookback=2D news_limit=5
finance price.context IOT date=2026-03-06 lookback=1W news_limit=5
notes: lookback is calendar time around date: 3D=3 calendar days before and after, 1W=7 calendar days, 1M=30 calendar days.
Timeline roles are temporal only: before_move, same_day, after_move.
Event/publication dates are explicit to avoid implied causal claims.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Price moves or dated evidence timeline.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads remote public/provider data. Does not mutate external state.

agent.use_when: Use when the user asks what filings, news, or transcripts were near a dated price move.
agent.avoid_when: Do not claim causality unless the evidence explicitly supports it.

commands.name: price.moves
commands.description: Find large deterministic close-to-close stock moves
commands.usage: finance price.moves SYMBOL [window=1d|3d|1w|1m years=3 threshold=8|8% limit=20 provider=auto]
examples: finance price.moves IOT years=3 threshold=8% limit=10
finance price.moves NVDA window=3d years=2 threshold=12%
finance price.moves NVDA window=1w years=2 threshold=15 limit=10
notes: window is a trading-day window: 1d=1 trading day, 1w=5 trading days, 1m=21 trading days.
threshold accepts decimal or percentage-point inputs: 0.08, 8, and 8% all mean 8%.
Uses one OHLCV fetch and deterministic close-to-close math.
Returns move dates and magnitude only; it does not infer causality.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Price moves or dated evidence timeline.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: Reads remote public/provider data. Does not mutate external state.

agent.use_when: Use for price move discovery and evidence timelines around a date.
agent.avoid_when: Do not infer causality from price.context alone.

commands.name: research.plan
commands.description: Return a deterministic research command checklist
notes: This returns suggested commands only; it does not execute research or form conclusions.
Use this as a navigation layer for repeatable research workflows.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Research checklist.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: No network, no filesystem read, no mutation.

agent.use_when: Use before executing a complex public-company research workflow.

commands.name: valuation.dcf
commands.description: Calculate DCF enterprise value from forecast cash flows
notes: Pass forecast FCF only; do not include an initial t=0 investment cash flow.
Forecast cash flows are discounted from t=1.
Use either terminal_growth or exit_multiple, not both.

cashflows.description: Forecast cash flows. CLI accepts K/M/B suffixes.

discount_rate.description: Discount rate; CLI accepts decimal or percent.

exit_multiple.description: Exit multiple alternative to terminal growth.

cashflows.description: Forecast cash flows. CLI accepts K/M/B suffixes.

discount_rate.description: Discount rate; CLI accepts decimal or percent.

exit_multiple.description: Exit multiple alternative to terminal growth.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Valuation calculation result and assumptions.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: No network, no filesystem read, no mutation.

agent.use_when: Use only for deterministic DCF math from explicit cash flows and discount assumptions.
agent.avoid_when: Do not use as investment advice or infer assumptions silently.

commands.name: valuation.irr
commands.description: Calculate IRR for periodic cash flows
notes: Cash flows are periodic; returns null with a warning when no IRR solution is bracketed.

cashflows.description: Cash-flow series. CLI accepts comma-separated K/M/B suffixed values.
Cash-flow series. CLI accepts comma-separated K/M/B suffixed values.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Valuation calculation result and assumptions.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: No network, no filesystem read, no mutation.

agent.use_when: Use for deterministic valuation math once assumptions are explicit.
agent.avoid_when: Do not present valuation output as investment advice.

commands.name: valuation.scenario
commands.description: Build a simple bull/base/bear sales-multiple scenario table
commands.usage: finance valuation.scenario SYMBOL revenue=2.2B bear_multiple=7 base_multiple=10 bull_multiple=13 [shares=580M]
examples: finance valuation.scenario IOT revenue=2.2B bear_multiple=7 base_multiple=10 bull_multiple=13 shares=580M
finance valuation.scenario IOT revenue=2200000000 bear_multiple=7 base_multiple=10 bull_multiple=13
notes: Uses current quote for price/share count when available; assumptions remain user-supplied.
Revenue and shares accept raw numbers or K/M/B suffixes.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Valuation calculation result and assumptions.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: No network, no filesystem read, no mutation.

agent.use_when: Use for deterministic valuation math once assumptions are explicit.
agent.avoid_when: Do not present valuation output as investment advice.

commands.name: valuation.wacc
commands.description: Calculate weighted average cost of capital
commands.usage: finance valuation.wacc equity_value=10B debt_value=1B cost_of_equity=10% cost_of_debt=5% tax_rate=21%
examples: finance valuation.wacc equity_value=10B debt_value=1B cost_of_equity=10% cost_of_debt=5% tax_rate=21%
notes: Formula: E/(D+E)*Re + D/(D+E)*Rd*(1-tax). Inputs are user-supplied.

tax_rate.description: Tax rate; CLI accepts decimal or percent.
Tax rate; CLI accepts decimal or percent.

ok.description: True when the command completed successfully.

anyOf.description: Valuation calculation result and assumptions.

error.description: Human-readable error when ok is false.

commands.side_effects_description: No network, no filesystem read, no mutation.

agent.use_when: Use for deterministic valuation math once assumptions are explicit.
agent.avoid_when: Do not present valuation output as investment advice.
